idea: Multi-Latent Attention (MLA): low-rank KV compression via latent bottleneck, reduces VRAM allowing larger batch or model
evidence: DeepSeek-V2/V3 paper; 93% KV cache reduction; multiple open-source implementations available
notes: Compresses K,V through shared low-rank latent. Frees VRAM headroom. Main benefit is inference but training VRAM savings could allow batch_size=8 or deeper model.

idea: NorMuon: normalize Muon update by applying norm step to attention params at stacked shape before reshape
evidence: modded-nanogpt PR#144 (-15 steps/-0.7s WR); note: "adding negligible value to gates and attention" per discussion
notes: Small change to Muon step. Low risk, low reward. Try after architecture changes.

idea: Learnable attention temperature: add per-layer scalar to scale QK dot product (exp(temp) * base_scale). QKnorm makes attention too diffuse.
evidence: nanochat autoresearch found QKnorm scaler multiplier was missing - attention was too diffuse. One of ~20 additive improvements.
notes: Simple change: one scalar per layer, init 0 (no change). Directly addresses known issue. Could sharpen attention where needed.

idea: Value embeddings weight decay: add WD specifically to value_emb while keeping WD=0 for everything else
evidence: nanochat autoresearch found 'Value Embeddings really like regularization and none was being applied'. We have WD=0 globally.
notes: Low risk. Only adds WD to one param group. Follows nanochat finding. VE may overfit while main model benefits from no WD.

idea: Tune AdamW betas for embedding/head groups: try (0.9, 0.95) or (0.85, 0.99) instead of current (0.8, 0.95)
evidence: nanochat autoresearch found 'AdamW betas were all messed up'. Current (0.8, 0.95) may not be optimal.
notes: 320 experiments were run just to tune beta1 in modded-nanogpt. High sensitivity suggests room for improvement.
